pi_items.metadata_value: ImageJ sample gel electrophoresis image (real laboratory gel)
pi_items.verified_value: ImageJ sample gel image, available at imagej.net/images/gel.gif as a sample for educational/training purposes
pi_items.source: Direct download and inspection of gel.gif from imagej.net/images/gel.gif; ImageJ documentation wiki references sample gel images in File > Open Samples menu; gel.gif file confirmed at imagej.net dated 2017-01-14

pi_items.verified_value: SDS-PAGE protein gel (gel analysis is confirmed as standard technique in ImageJ for SDS-PAGE gels)
pi_items.source: Multiple sources confirm ImageJ supports SDS-PAGE gel analysis: lukemiller.org, protocols.io, signagen.com, imagejdocu.list.lu; gel image structure consistent with SDS-PAGE
PIL/Pillow analysis of downloaded gel.gif: Image.mode='L' (8-bit), Format='GIF', Size=(276,467)
URL testing: imagej.nih.gov/ij/images/gel.gif returns HTTP 404 (Not Found); imagej.net/images/gel.gif returns HTTP 200 (OK); setup_task.sh uses imagej.net as fallback URL
Direct file download and measurement; imagej.net directory listing shows 88K file size
imagej.net directory listing and HTTP headers from gel.gif download

No official documentation found confirming or specifying the staining method of gel.gif; multiple sources confirm Coomassie blue is standard for SDS-PAGE gels, but specific staining of this sample image not documented

pi_items.metadata_value: The gel.gif image is a classic real laboratory gel that has been part of the ImageJ sample dataset for decades and is widely used for teaching gel analysis (from README.md)
pi_items.verified_value: gel.gif is a sample gel image in the ImageJ sample library used for educational/training purposes
pi_items.source: ImageJ documentation wiki confirms sample gel available in File > Open Samples menu; gel.gif confirmed as part of imagej.net sample images; no sources found describing it as actual experimental data; ImageJ documentation refers to it as a 'sample' for learning
Task.json specifies reference_lane as 1; this is a task design choice, not a claim about the data itself

pi_items.metadata_value: Multiple protein samples separated by molecular weight across several lanes
pi_items.verified_value: Image contains multiple vertical regions/lanes with darker bands on lighter background, consistent with gel electrophoresis structure
pi_items.source: Visual analysis of gel.gif: image shows visible horizontal/vertical band structures in different intensity regions, consistent with protein separation in electrophoresis lanes

privileged_info_summary: The gel.gif image is confirmed to be an 8-bit grayscale GIF file (276x467 pixels) from the ImageJ sample library, available at imagej.net/images/gel.gif (not the claimed imagej.nih.gov URL, which returns 404). The image contains the characteristic band structure expected of SDS-PAGE protein gels and is suitable for gel densitometry analysis. However, the claim that it is a 'real laboratory gel' is not supported by official documentation—it is presented as a sample image for educational purposes in the ImageJ software. The specific staining method (Coomassie blue) is not definitively documented.
